subject: concept:epistemic-vigilance
quote: openness and vigilance evolved hand in hand as human communication became increasingly broad and powerful... If our more recent and sophisticated cognitive machinery is disrupted, we revert to our conservative core
rationale: 'Open vigilance' is paired-skill (openness+vigilance); the catalog's epistemic-vigilance flattens this into one-sided defense.

subject: concept:epistemic-vigilance
quote: people rely on two main mechanisms: plausibility checking, which compares the content of the message with our preexisting beliefs, and reasoning, which checks whether arguments offered in support of the message resonate
rationale: Plausibility-checking + reasoning (argumentation) are Mercier's two named sub-mechanisms; no catalog entries for either.

subject: concept:paradox-of-virality
quote: Believing something... is not an all-or-nothing matter... A belief can remain essentially inert... Dan Sperber has called such beliefs reflective, by contrast with intuitive beliefs, from which we freely draw inferences
rationale: Sperber's reflective/intuitive belief distinction explains shared-but-not-endorsed; the paradox concept has no anchor for it.

subject: concept:manipulation-surface
quote: Self-incriminating statements are intrinsically credible... We shouldn't assume that people intuitively hold the apparently deranged or evil views they profess.
rationale: 'Burning bridges' / unclubbable signaling: extreme views as coalition-signals, not beliefs. No catalog anchor.

subject: concept:manipulation-surface
quote: the population seek to justify making misguided or evil decisions that they hold false beliefs... we not only spontaneously justify ourselves when our behavior is questioned but also learn to anticipate when justifications might be needed
rationale: The 'justification market' — false beliefs as post hoc rationalization — reverses the project's belief→behavior arrow. No catalog anchor.

subject: concept:bridge-zone
quote: Dan Sperber has suggested that, in unusual circumstances, obscurity can become a strength through a 'guru effect.'... when hard-to-understand content spreads, it is not because it is obscure but in spite of being obscure
rationale: 'Guru effect' — obscurity flipping into authority via reputation credit — is a distinctive bridge-zone failure mode.

subject: concept:bridge-zone
quote: We give a kind of reputation credit. For information to be deemed valuable, it must be both plausible and useful... information about threats has the potential to be very useful
rationale: 'Reputation credit' — pre-verification competence attribution — explains how unverifiable bridge sources accumulate authority.

subject: concept:epistemic-vigilance
quote: the general logic of open vigilance mechanisms suggests that, on the whole, we make more errors of omission (not trusting when we should) than of commission (trusting when we shouldn't)
rationale: Vigilance is calibrated toward refusal not acceptance; the catalog's entry doesn't anchor this directionality.

subject: concept:handle-ability
quote: One is mathematizing: if math is used, then the outcome is more likely to be thought of as good science... Another coarse cue is proximity to the hard sciences... An even coarser cue is the prestige of a university
rationale: Coarse cues (math, prestige, credentials) are handle-ability's selection-side cousins for novice receivers. No catalog anchor.

subject: mechanism:selection
quote: there is room for what Guillaume Dezecache, Thom Scott-Phillips, and I have called emotional vigilance—mechanisms of open vigilance dedicated to emotional signals
rationale: 'Emotional vigilance' is the sub-mechanism the project's Ch.7 floor argument has to defeat — no catalog anchor.

subject: concept:credibility-weighting
quote: what matters isn't the cost of buying the new iPhone per se but the fact that spending so much money on a phone is costlier for a poor person... costly signaling can, counterintuitively, make a signal reliable even if no cost is paid
rationale: Differential-cost-to-unreliable-signaler keeps cheap talk honest; the catalog has no anchor for this mechanism.

subject: concept:false-consensus-rational-agents
quote: Each individual testimony would have been unconvincing (I assume you don't believe in every god of every religious person you have ever talked to), but the aggregate makes for a very persuasive package
rationale: 'Circular reporting' / independence-illusion is the operational mechanism for false consensus among rationals.

_note: Most load-bearing missing concept: Sperber's reflective/intuitive belief split — underwrites Mercier's reading of paradox-of-virality, false rumors, conspiracy theories, witches' confessions, absurd flattery. Mercier's 'open vigilance' is a paired construct (openness+vigilance evolved together) the catalog's epistemic-vigilance entry flattens. Justification market / post-hoc rationalization reverses the belief→behavior arrow the project assumes. Burning bridges / unclubbable signaling, guru effect, reputation credit, circular reporting, emotional vigilance, coarse cues are all distinct mechanisms without anchors. Vigilance's omission>commission asymmetry contradicts the project's 'too gullible' framing — worth a foundational note.
